description:
You are a monitoring specialist who has just finished testing a new alert-rule file.  
The rule now has to be activated by moving it from the “staging” area into the live “enabled” directory that the monitoring daemon watches.

Initial state
1. A directory called /home/user/staging_rules/ already exists.  
2. Inside that directory there is exactly one file named critical_load.rule whose contents are:

alert: cpu_load
severity: critical
threshold: 0.85

3. The target directory /home/user/monitoring/rules/enabled/ already exists and is writable by your user.

Goal
• The file /home/user/staging_rules/critical_load.rule must be moved (not copied) into /home/user/monitoring/rules/enabled/.  
• After the task finishes:

  - /home/user/monitoring/rules/enabled/critical_load.rule must exist and contain the three lines shown above, byte-for-byte.  
  - /home/user/staging_rules/critical_load.rule must no longer be present.  

How your solution will be checked
1. The grader will verify that the destination file exists with exactly the same three-line content (no extra new-lines or spaces).  
2. It will confirm that the source file path no longer exists.  

Important formatting rule for your terminal output
Immediately after performing the move, list the contents of the destination directory so the human observer can see the result.  
Print exactly the output of the command:

ls -1 /home/user/monitoring/rules/enabled/

(That is, one file name per line, no flags other than “-1”).  
No other terminal output is required for the test, but additional commentary is fine; the automated grader, however, only checks the filesystem.

Constraint
Accomplish the move with a single terminal command.
truth:
Filesystem before the agent starts:
• Directory /home/user/staging_rules/
  ‑ File /home/user/staging_rules/critical_load.rule  
    Content (11 bytes + newlines):
      alert: cpu_load\n
      severity: critical\n
      threshold: 0.85\n

• Directory /home/user/monitoring/rules/enabled/   (empty)

Required final state after correct completion:
• /home/user/monitoring/rules/enabled/critical_load.rule exists with identical 3-line content.  
• /home/user/staging_rules/critical_load.rule path does not exist (neither file nor symlink nor directory).  
• No additional files are created anywhere else.

The grader’s checks:
1. test -f "/home/user/monitoring/rules/enabled/critical_load.rule"
2. diff -q "/home/user/monitoring/rules/enabled/critical_load.rule" <(printf 'alert: cpu_load\nseverity: critical\nthreshold: 0.85\n')
3. test ! -e "/home/user/staging_rules/critical_load.rule"
name: task_000000_49159749
